title: Global citizenship and sustainable development education
subtitle: An index from 0 (worst) to 1 (best) measuring the extent to which global citizenship and sustainable development education are mainstreamed into different strands of education: national education policies, student assessment, teacher education, and education curricula.
caption:
Global citizenship and sustainable development education, 2020

An index from 0 (worst) to 1 (best) measuring the extent to which global citizenship and sustainable development education are mainstreamed into different strands of education: national education policies, student assessment, teacher education, and education curricula.

The chart presents horizontal bar charts for four countries—Colombia, India, Malawi, and Bulgaria—showing their scores across four education strands in 2020. Each strand is scored on a scale from 0 to 1, with 1 representing full mainstreaming. Colombia scores 1 in national education policies and student assessment, 0.88 in education curricula, and 0.85 in teacher education. India also scores 1 in national education policies and student assessment, 0.95 in teacher education, and 0.92 in education curricula. Malawi matches Colombia and India with 1 in national education policies and student assessment, 0.91 in education curricula, and 0.9 in teacher education. Bulgaria’s scores are lower across all strands: 0.73 in teacher education, 0.71 in student assessment, 0.65 in education curricula, and 0.56 in national education policies—the lowest single score shown. The indicators are based on self-reporting by countries, though countries are asked to provide supporting evidence to back up their responses. Across the four countries, national education policies and student assessment receive the highest scores in three cases, while Bulgaria shows its weakest performance in national education policies. Teacher education and education curricula show more variation, with India scoring highest in teacher education and Colombia lowest among the four in that strand. The most visible pattern is that three countries achieve maximum scores in at least two strands, while Bulgaria’s scores fall below 0.75 in every category.
